lintExempt.rationale: Re-affirmed 2026-04-15 (task #22 audit, post-v0.20.0 / post-PR #208). These phase JSONs are shipped historical execution records of PH-01 through PH-04 work; no automated runtime consumer exists (verified: zero grep hits in `server/**`, zero references in `.github/workflows/ci.yml`, zero usage in test suites, ac-lint is a pure function library that only runs on caller-supplied plans). The only voluntary consumer is `forge_lint_refresh planPath=<this-file>`, which is an audit-time operation against a specific path and would only re-surface pre-existing F-55/F-56 violations describing already-shipped work. Live drift in newly authored plans is prevented by `npm run lint` (CI-enforced via PR #208) and per-call ac-lint, not by retroactive cleanup of historical records. Original C1-bootstrap "we'll unwind later" framing superseded — exemption retained as the correct end-state for shipped artifacts. Unwind via `grep -l 2026-04-13-c1-bootstrap .ai-workspace/plans/*.json` only if a future runtime consumer is added that would load these files automatically.

stories.title: Wire RunContext with ProgressReporter and AuditLog for forge_generate (REQ-12)

acceptanceCriteria.description: RunContext created with toolName 'forge_generate' and stages for init/iterate
ProgressReporter stages reported: at minimum 'init' or 'iterate'
AuditLog entry written with the action taken (implement/fix/escalate)
CostTracker records $0 cost (no API calls) but is wired for aggregation
Audit file created at .forge/audit/forge_generate-*.jsonl after call with projectPath
After forge_generate call with projectPath, .forge/runs/data.jsonl contains a new JSONL line
Each JSONL line contains timestamp, storyId, iteration, action, score, and durationMs
acceptanceCriteria.command: npx vitest run server/lib/generator.test.ts -t 'JSONL.*fields\|JSONL.*timestamp\|run record.*fields' 2>&1 | grep -q 'passed'

acceptanceCriteria.description: JSONL file is append-only (multiple calls add lines, never overwrite)
When projectPath is not set, no JSONL is written and no error occurs
JSONL write failure does not block the core brief/decision response (NFR-05)

stories.title: Implement cost estimation output with character-count heuristic (REQ-16)

acceptanceCriteria.description: Result includes costEstimate with briefTokens computed as character_count / 4
projectedIterationCostUsd estimates based on brief size and Opus pricing
Cost estimation failure does not block core response (NFR-05)
For Claude Code Max users (no API calls), projectedIterationCostUsd reflects $0 with a note about coordinator API costs
